title: stapelia rufescens
intro: Stapelia rufescens, also known as the red-headed starfish or red-headed toadstool cactus, is a succulent plant native to South Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of certain bacteria and fungi. The health benefits of Stapelia rufescens include its potential to treat skin infections, wounds, and other bacterial and fungal diseases. Herbal preparations of Stapelia rufescens are typically made into ointments, creams, or tinctures that can be applied topically to affected areas.
